title: Fix public download links routing and auto-download behavior

requirements.summary: Make copied public download links use the correct path-based route (/d/$token) so opening them loads PublicDownloadPage and downloads work, with clear invalid-token errors.
acceptanceCriteria: The copied public link points to a URL that routes to the PublicDownloadPage ("/d/$token") in the deployed app, not a URL that leaves the app on the home route with an unhandled hash fragment.
Opening the copied link in a new tab shows the PublicDownloadPage and successfully downloads the file via actor.downloadFile(token).
If the token is invalid, the user sees an English error message on the PublicDownloadPage.
The existing Copy Link actions in both the user file list and admin file list produce working links.

file_operations.description: Update buildPublicDownloadUrl(token) to generate path-based URLs using the app origin and "/d/<token>" (remove the "#/" hash fragment) so copied links route to the existing TanStack Router route (/d/$token).

Improve invalid-token handling to always show a clear English error message by using the existing error normalization utility (normalizeError) rather than raw error.message, while keeping the page on the /d/$token route.

requirements.summary: Auto-start downloads once when PublicDownloadPage loads with a valid token, while preserving a manual Download button fallback and clear loading/success states.
acceptanceCriteria: When navigating to "/d/<token>", the page automatically initiates the download once (no infinite retries).
While downloading, the page displays a clear loading state; after triggering the browser download, it displays a success state.
The manual Download button remains available and works if auto-download fails or is cancelled.

file_operations.description: Add an auto-download effect that triggers download(token) once when the page loads and the actor is ready (use the existing useActor hook to gate the auto-run); ensure it does not re-trigger on rerenders (e.g., via a ref/flag) and keep the manual Download button functional as a fallback with existing loading/success UI states.
